constraints_1_content: Before writing solution.sh, use Bash to explore the /app/projects directory tree first: `find /app/projects -maxdepth 3 -not -type l | head -40` to understand the real project layout and file distribution before coding any parsing logic.
constraints_1_rationale: This is a ClaudeCode Bash tool usage pattern specific to this file-scanning task; it wouldn't apply to unrelated domains.
constraints_2_content: Use the Read tool to inspect at least one real sample of each dependency file format (requirements.txt, package.json, Cargo.toml) found under /app/projects before writing any parsing code, to catch unexpected formatting quirks early.
constraints_2_rationale: Directly references the Read tool and is specific to this multi-format dependency parsing task.
constraints_3_content: After writing solution.sh, validate it end-to-end by running `bash /app/solution.sh` via Bash, then immediately use Bash to check both CSV files exist and their first lines match the exact required headers before declaring success.
constraints_3_rationale: References the Bash tool for validation and is specific to this CSV-generation task.
constraints_7_content: Use TodoWrite to outline the implementation plan (explore → parse requirements.txt → parse package.json → parse Cargo.toml → aggregate → compute severity/resolvability → write CSVs) before writing any code, so each phase can be checked off incrementally.
constraints_7_rationale: References the TodoWrite tool; the multi-phase structure is specific to this complex multi-format parsing task.
